channel.title: 2. Introducing Yourself on Italiano con Geoff
channel.description: Recent content in 2. Introducing Yourself on Italiano con Geoff

item.title: Lesson 2.1: How to Say Your Name
item.description:
Lesson 2.1: How to Say Your Name – Introductions Made Easy 👋🇮🇹 #

Knowing how to introduce yourself is a must in any language. In this lesson, you’ll learn how to confidently say your name, ask others for theirs, and maybe even learn some fun cultural tidbits along the way. Ready to start? Let’s dive in!

What You’ll Learn #

By the end of this lesson, you’ll be able to:

item.title: Exercise 2.1: Introducing Yourself Practice
item.description:
Exercise 2.1: Introducing Yourself Practice – Say Hello, Make Friends 👋 #

Let’s put those introduction phrases into practice with fresh scenarios that make sense and help you feel confident in any setting.

1. Fill in the Blanks – Introduce Yourself #

Complete the dialogue by filling in the blanks with the correct Italian phrases.

Scenario: Geoff is meeting Viktor at a café for the first time.

  1. Geoff: Ciao! _____ (My name is) Geoff. _____ (What’s your name?)
    Viktor: Ciao! Mi chiamo Viktor. _____ (Nice to meet you!), Geoff!

item.title: Lesson 2.2: Talking About Your Hobbies and Interests
item.description:
Lesson 2.2: Talking About Your Hobbies and Interests – What Do You Love to Do? 🎸🎨 #

Talking about hobbies and interests is a great way to connect with people in any language. In this lesson, you’ll learn how to share what you enjoy doing, ask about others’ interests, and even sneak in your job title—because who doesn’t like talking about themselves just a little?

What You’ll Learn #

By the end of this lesson, you’ll be able to:

item.title: Exercise 2.2: Talking About Your Hobbies and Interests
item.description:
Exercise 2.2: Talking About Your Hobbies and Interests 🎸🎨 #

Let’s put your knowledge to the test! This exercise will help you practice talking about hobbies, asking about interests, and even sharing your profession. Don’t worry—Geoff and Viktor are here to make learning fun!

1. Fill in the Blanks – Complete the Sentence #

Complete each sentence with the correct Italian phrase.

  1. Geoff: Cosa ti piace fare, Viktor?
    Viktor: Mi piace fare escursioni e leggere libri. E a te, Geoff?
    Geoff: Mi piace guardare film e _____. (cucinare / lavorare / studiare) (Hint: Geoff loves cooking and reading, but working? Not so much.)
